item.title: Version 1.4.3 — Fn+Delete works again, plus a friendlier push-to-talk
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.4.3 — Fn keys behave the way macOS intended

Two small but meaningful keyboard fixes for anyone who uses Abalone with Fn as their dictation key.

  • Fn+Delete forward-deletes again. When Abalone intercepts the Fn key for dictation, it accidentally also blocked macOS from translating Fn+Delete into a forward delete. Same for Fn+Left/Right (Home/End) and Fn+Up/Down (Page Up/Down). All of those now work the way they should.
  • Holding Fn with another key won't accidentally start recording. Previously, if you held Fn for forward-deletes (or any Fn-modifier action), the hold timer could fire and start dictation behind your back. Now the moment you press another key while holding Fn, Abalone knows you're using Fn as a modifier — recording stays off.
  • Push-to-talk feels more deliberate. The hold threshold is now 400ms (up from 150ms) — matching the double-tap window. A casual brush of Fn won't trigger PTT, but a clear hold still feels snappy.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.4.2 — polish: cursor feedback, mic default, final-word transcription fix
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.4.2

A polish release — no new features, just things that should have been right from the start.

What's changed

  • Your Mac's built-in mic is now always the default. Previously the app could silently switch to a Bluetooth device (like AirPods), causing a 1–3 second connection delay before your first dictation went through. Now Abalone always records from the built-in microphone unless you explicitly choose something else in Settings → Microphone.
  • "Connecting mic…" feedback. If you do choose a Bluetooth mic and it needs a moment to negotiate, the dictation pill now shows "Connecting mic…" instead of a silent waveform while it waits.
  • Final words no longer get cut off. Short final words at the end of a dictation ("…and finally") were sometimes dropped. Fixed by giving the audio engine a moment to flush its last buffer before transcription runs.
  • Pointer cursor everywhere. Every button, toggle, keycap, and menu item in the app now shows the hand cursor on hover. No more arrow cursor over things you can click.

No changes to core dictation, shortcuts, your dictionary, or cloud sync.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.4.1 — dock icon brings the window back reliably
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.4.1

A small but long-overdue fix: clicking the Abalone Flow icon in your Dock now reliably brings the main window back, even after you've closed it.

What's changed

  • Dock-click always reopens the window. Previously, after closing the main window with the red ✕, clicking the Dock icon sometimes did nothing. The window now hides instead of being destroyed, and a single Dock click brings it forward every time.
  • Window follows you across Spaces. If the main window is on a different desktop (Space) than the one you're currently on, clicking the Dock icon now pulls the window to your current Space — instead of yanking you to wherever the window was sitting.
  • Minimized windows un-minimize on Dock click. If you'd minimized the window to the Dock with the yellow –, clicking the app icon now restores it directly.

No changes to dictation, transcription, shortcuts, or your dictionary. Same shortcuts, same keys, same everything else.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.4.0 — Abalone learns proper nouns from your edits
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.4.0 — Adaptive Dictionary

Abalone now learns the words you care about. Names, products, jargon — anything Whisper gets wrong the first time. You fix it once; from then on, it's right.

How it works

  • Auto-learn from your corrections. When you fix a transcription — change "Wispr" to "Wispr Flow", or "Sameer" to "Samir" — Abalone quietly notes the corrected proper noun. Next time you dictate, Whisper biases toward your spelling.
  • Add words yourself. Open Settings → Dictionary and type the words you want Abalone to know: company names, teammates, product lines. Up to 100 entries, on-device only.
  • Source badge per entry. Words you typed in show as "added by you"; words Abalone caught from your edits show with a sparkle ✨ "learned" badge — so you can tell which ones came from where.
  • One toggle to disable. If auto-learn isn't your thing, flip the switch at the top of the Dictionary page. Manual entries keep working.

What triggers a learning

Auto-learn waits for a clear "I'm done editing" signal before saving anything. The strongest signals are pressing Enter to send (in Slack, ChatGPT, Cursor, etc.) and switching apps. Clicking a Send button with the mouse, or focusing a different field, also counts. The system never learns from text you typed — only from edits you made to dictated text.

What gets filtered out

To avoid learning typos or junk, Abalone runs every candidate word through Apple's spell-checker plus a stoplist of common English words. Only proper nouns and words that look like real names make it into your dictionary. Lowercase corrections, common-word swaps, and partial-word edits are skipped.

Privacy: the dictionary lives on your Mac. If you're signed in, it syncs to your account so it's there on your next device — same shape as your transcription history.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.3.2 — shortcuts settings polish, one-tap error fixes
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.3.2

The Shortcuts page in Settings got a cleanup. Same shortcuts, same keys — just easier to read, easier to fix, and easier to reset.

What's changed

  • Cleaner two-column layout. Each row now reads label on the left, keys on the right — the standard macOS settings convention. Keycaps scan cleanly down the right edge so you can spot what's bound to what at a glance.
  • One Reset all shortcuts button. A single button at the top-right of the page resets every shortcut to its default, appearing only when something's actually been changed. The old per-row reset is gone — one clear escape hatch instead of two.
  • One-tap fixes for invalid combos. If you try to bind something that won't work — a bare letter that fires while you type, Shift alone, or a universal ⌘ combo like ⌘Z — the error now shows tappable suggestion chips. Click one to commit a fixed version without re-recording.
  • "Double-tap for hands-free" moved into the description. The hint now sits alongside "Hold to record" under Push to Talk, where behavior copy belongs. The controls column is just keycaps and actions.

No changes to dictation, transcription, hotkey behavior, or anything outside the Settings page. Your bindings carry over unchanged.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.3.1 — card polish, reliable hover-to-pause
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.3.1

Small polish on the cards that drop down from the notch — the permission prompt, model-download error, and sign-up nudge. Nothing new, just nicer.

What's changed

  • Countdown ring around the X. Cards that auto-dismiss now show a thin ring that drains as the timer runs out, so you can see how long you have before it disappears.
  • Hover pauses the timer — reliably. Move the cursor anywhere over a card and the countdown freezes. Move away and it picks back up. (Previously this only worked sometimes, depending on where the cursor crossed.)
  • Roomier layout. The dismiss X now sits in its own column in the top-right corner, and there's more breathing room between the title, body, and button. The card still stays locked to the notch width so it reads as one stacked element with the notch above.

No changes to dictation, transcription, hotkeys, or anything else. Your settings and recordings carry over unchanged.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.3.0 — dictation moves to the notch
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.3.0

The dictation pill has moved. Instead of floating at the bottom of the screen, it now lives at the top — anchored to the notch, extending downward as a small card when you record. Closer to where your eyes already are, out of the way of whatever you're writing into, and finally giving the notch a job on Macs that have one.

What's new

  • Notch-anchored pill. The pill sits flush against the top edge of your screen, extending the hardware notch if you have one and sitting in the same spot on Macs without. Hover it to see the shortcut hint; click to start recording.
  • Undoable cancel. When you stop a recording by hitting the X, you get a short grace period with a progress bar and an Undo button. Hover the card and the countdown pauses — take as long as you need. Miss the window and the audio is discarded cleanly.
  • New recording animations. A small abalone pearl appears during warming-up, transcribing, and done states, with motion tuned to the phase the app is in. The waveform during recording is crisper and the completion "done" moment is a single clean checkmark.
  • New menu bar icon. A custom pearl-crescent template replaces the generic microphone glyph — matches the app's identity and respects your menu bar's light/dark appearance.

Everything else — hotkeys, transcription quality, cloud sync, history — behaves the same. Your settings and recordings carry over unchanged.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.2.4 — fix: rapid hotkey taps no longer spawn ghost recordings
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.2.4

Quiet but nasty bug fix. Rapid-fire-tapping your recording hotkey — five or six times in quick succession — could start multiple overlapping recording sessions in the background. Each would run invisibly until hitting the 10-minute max-duration cap, at which point it would paste a duplicate into your active app and save a near-empty 10-minute clip to your history. The pill would shimmer strangely and you'd often have to force-quit to escape. Fixed.

Change

  • Recording keys are ignored while a session is wrapping up. If you press the hotkey during the brief window where the app is transcribing and pasting the previous session, the press is dropped rather than starting a new overlapping one. You can start a fresh recording as soon as that finishes (usually under a second).

No behaviour changes to dictation, cloud sync, or telemetry.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.2.3 — smarter update checks + reliable restart
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.2.3

Closes out the auto-update flow. The "Restart to Update" button now reliably installs the update and relaunches the app. Previously, under certain conditions, clicking it would quit the app without installing anything — leaving you stuck on the old version.

Changes

  • Checks for updates every time you launch the app, plus once an hour while it's running. Previously it only checked once every 24 hours, which meant relaunching didn't necessarily trigger a fresh check.
  • "Restart to Update" now reliably installs. You'll see a brief one-click "Install and Relaunch" confirmation from Sparkle when you click the capsule — then the app installs and relaunches, the way it was always supposed to.

No behaviour changes to dictation, cloud sync, or telemetry.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.2.2 — stale-cache update CTA fix
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.2.2

Another small polish on the auto-update flow. In 1.2.1 a stale DMG cache could leave the "Restart to Update" capsule on-screen even when there was nothing new to install — clicking it would quit the app without doing anything. This release ignores Sparkle's delegate callbacks for versions that match what's already running.

Change

  • The "Restart to Update" capsule only appears when there's a genuinely newer build waiting. No more ghost CTAs that do nothing when clicked.

No behaviour changes to dictation, cloud sync, or telemetry.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.2.1 — silent auto-update fix
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.2.1

A quick follow-up to 1.2.0. The automatic update flow wasn't reliably surfacing the "Restart to Update" button when a new version was available in the background — you'd either see nothing, or occasionally a stray Sparkle dialog. This fixes both.

Change

  • Sparkle now downloads new versions silently in the background (as intended) and the "Restart to Update" capsule appears automatically under the sidebar logo, in Settings, and in the app menu — no dialogs to dismiss.

No behaviour changes to dictation, cloud sync, or telemetry from 1.2.0.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.2.0 — per-device telemetry + privacy policy
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.2.0

New: the app now reports non-sensitive device info when you sign in, so we can debug issues faster and understand what Macs and OS versions our users are on. Nothing changes in how dictation works — your voice is still transcribed locally on your Mac.

What gets reported

  • Mac model, chip, macOS version, app version, locale, timezone, and a stable hardware identifier — sent to our server the first time you sign in and on each launch afterwards.
  • IP address and country (derived by our edge provider) — logged for security monitoring.

What does not get collected

  • No precise location, no contact lists, no browsing history, no data from other apps.
  • No selling, sharing, or licensing of your data. No ad networks. No AI training on what you say.

Full details, including how to request deletion, are in our privacy policy. If you'd rather not share this data, email vincent@vincentwei.co and we'll delete your account on request.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.1.3 — silent Restart to Update
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.1.3

Smoother update flow — no more dialogs to click through.

Change

  • "Restart to Update" is now silent. Clicking the button used to surface two Sparkle dialogs ("Update Available" and then "Install and Relaunch"). Now it just quits the app, installs the new version, and relaunches — no clicks needed.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.1.2 — launch fix for China
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.1.2

Fixes a launch failure reported by users in mainland China.

Fix

  • "Apple could not verify" dialog on first launch. The notarization ticket is now stapled to the app bundle itself, so Gatekeeper can verify offline — no need to reach Apple's servers. This unblocks users in regions where Apple's notarization endpoints are throttled.

No other behavior changes. Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.1.1 — bug-fix release
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.1.1

Bug fixes for home stats, hands-free, and push-to-talk.

Fixes

  • Home stats from cloud DB. Weekly streak and 7-day WPM now read from the full cloud dataset, not the 200-row local cache.
  • Modifier-only hands-free fires instantly. No more 150 ms hold before Control+Command (or any modifier combo bound to hands-free) starts recording.
  • Push-to-talk no longer hangs after a mode switch. Pressing push-to-talk to stop hands-free used to silently break the key until a restart.
  • Double-tap to re-enter hands-free works every time. After stopping hands-free with a single tap, the next double-tap was being dropped.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.1.0 — Richer mid-session mode switching
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.1.0

Better mid-session control, more configurable hotkeys, fewer false scolds.

New hotkey gestures

  • Latch mid-session: Hold your PTT key, then press your hands-free combo — recording continues, now in hands-free.
  • Double-tap modifier-only PTT: E.g. Control alone, now toggles hands-free. Used to only work on Fn.
  • Single-tap to stop hands-free: Press your PTT key once during an active session to stop and transcribe.
  • Extend the chord: Ctrl → Ctrl+Cmd during PTT promotes into hands-free. Works with any binding you configure.
  • Confirmation sound: A short "Pop" plays on mode flips so you always know what state you're in.

Fixes

  • Hotkeys attach at HID driver level — other dictation apps can't steal your keys.
  • Accidental short taps (<200 ms) no longer trigger the "No speech detected" warning.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon.

item.title: Version 1.0.0 — Speak 3x faster than you type. Anywhere on your Mac.
item.description:
Abalone Flow 1.0.0

Speak 3x faster than you type. Anywhere on your Mac.

You speak at 140 words per minute. You type at 40. Abalone Flow closes that gap everywhere you'd normally type — Mail, Slack, Claude Code, Cursor, Obsidian, Linear, Notion.

Hold a key. Speak. Release. Your words land at the cursor, already punctuated and capitalized, before you've taken your hand off the key.

The five things that matter

  • Transcription runs on your Mac. OpenAI Whisper via WhisperKit, native on Apple Silicon. Audio never leaves your machine.
  • Your history syncs across devices. Transcribed text follows you from laptop to desktop; audio stays local.
  • One-click sign-in with Google. No forms, no email to verify, no inbox ceremony.
  • Free. No subscription, no credit card, no word limits today.
  • Near-perfect accuracy. ~97% on clean English. Swap in Large-v3 for broadcast-grade transcription.

Requires macOS 14 Sonoma or later. Apple Silicon (M1, M2, M3, M4).
